work.title: WORK
proyect.title: Clínica Vitalis
proyecto.resum: Hallucinate is the fourth single from the album Future Nostalgia, released by the singer Dua Lipa in 2020. The idea was to make a colourful and psychedelic illustration to induce an hallucinatory effect. The various elements around the portrait are made in reference to the lyrics and more generally to the universe of the album as well as the disco-house instrumental of the single.
proyecto.resum.Guarderia: DayCare is a web platform developed for the comprehensive management of childcare centers, focused on optimizing administrative processes and improving communication between users and the institution. The system allows the management of users, roles, enrollments, appointments, and educational programs within a secure and organized environment. It was built using frontend technologies such as HTML, CSS, and JavaScript for a modern and responsive interface, along with PHP for the backend and MySQL as the database. The main goal is to centralize information, automate key tasks, and provide an efficient experience for both administrators and users.
proyecto.resum.MPG: Muebles Palmares Gera (MPG) is a web platform designed for the display and promotion of modern furniture, focused on delivering an elegant and visually engaging experience. The site showcases collections for living rooms, dining rooms, bedrooms, and wardrobes, using smooth animations, minimalistic design, and an intuitive structure. It was developed using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, prioritizing performance, responsiveness, and image optimization. The main goal is to provide seamless navigation, highlight product quality, and facilitate direct customer contact through integrations such as WhatsApp.
about.title: ABOUT
about.resum: Software Engineering student focused on building real, scalable systems. I work with Java (Spring Boot), PHP, MySQL and C#, developing structured applications with clean architecture and strong database design. Beyond backend development, I care deeply about UI/UX, motion and detail — because software shouldn’t just work, it should feel right. I’m constantly building, refining and pushing my skills forward.
